member.name: Karin Smyth
member.synopsis: Karin Smyth is the Labour MP for Bristol South , and has been an MP continually since 7 May 2015. She currently holds the Government post of Minister of State (Department of Health and Social Care).

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Tomography
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, how many and what proportion of (a) CT and (b) MRI scanners have been in operation for longer than 10 years.
writtenQuestions.answerText: It has not proved possible to respond to the hon. Member in the time available before Dissolution.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: NHS Trusts: ICT
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, how many IT failures there have been at NHS trusts that affected clinical service delivery in each of the last five years.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The information requested is not held centrally.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: NHS Trusts: Buildings
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, in how many NHS trusts temporary buildings are in use.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The National Health Service does not hold data on the number of temporary buildings in use.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: NHS Trusts: Infrastructure
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, how many infrastructure failures at NHS trusts have been recorded in each of the last five years; and how many such failures had an impact on clinical service delivery in each of those years.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The National Health Service publishes the annual Estates Returns Information Collection, which includes detailed information on the NHS estate. Individual NHS Trusts are responsible for providing the data for the Estates Returns Information Collection and...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Integrated Care Systems: Finance

writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department does not hold the information requested. Commissioning Support Units (CSUs) form part of one service provided to Clinical Commissioning Groups and integrated care boards by NHS England. CSUs are not separate legal entities, and spending for...

writtenQuestions.heading: Health Services
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, whether she has made an assessment of progress in the development of a clinical analytical service for specialised commissioning.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Commissioners of Specialised Services have access to the analysis of clinical data via their regional business intelligence teams, or the nationally commissioned Commissioning Support Unit (CSU) service provision.Routine reporting can be accessed via the ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Health Services
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps she is taking to ensure that integrated care boards take into account a) clinical, b) patient and c) provider experience when commissioning specialised services.
writtenQuestions.answerText: NHS England's delegation agreement with the integrated care boards (ICBs) requires each ICB to establish effective, safe, efficient, and economic arrangements for the commissioning of delegated specialised services, with particular reference to statutory ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.answerText: The nine joint committee arrangements arose from a robust process, which included a readiness assessment, the Pre-Delegation Assessment Framework, made between integrated care boards (ICBs) and NHS England regional teams, followed by a National Moderation...

writtenQuestions.heading: Integrated Care Boards
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what assessment has she made of the adequacy of the progress towards full delegated commissioning for Integrated Care Boards by April 2025 in (a) the South West, (b) the South East, (c) London and

writtenQuestions.heading: PA Consulting Group: Contracts
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, with reference to the contract awarded by her Department to PA Consulting Services Limited on 8 January 2024 under procurement reference CF-2104, what the estimated cost to the public purse will be
writtenQuestions.answerText: Procurement CF-2014 is for digital information services to support the work of the National Institute for Health and Care Research. The award amount is available at the following link: https://www.contractsfinder.service.gov.uk/Notice/9fb00b7d-b2dd-4672-a...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Hospitals: Construction
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps she is taking to improve staff retention on the New Hospitals Programme.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The New Hospital Programme (NHP) continues to recruit into outstanding vacancies and is making progress in line with its current plans, workforce planning and resource assumptions.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Hospitals: Construction
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what steps she is taking to reduce vacancies on the New Hospitals Programme.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The New Hospital Programme (NHP) continues to recruit into outstanding vacancies and is making progress in line with its current plans, workforce planning and resource assumptions.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care
